name: Winter Vegetable Potage
recipeIngredient: 1 small Chili (jalapeno, split lengthwise, seeded, and chopped)
2 medium Potatoes (baking, peeled and coarsely chopped)
1 medium Celery Root (celeriac, pared and coarsely chopped, optional)
recipeInstructions:
See recipes for Chicken Stock.
1. In a large saucepan or casserole, melt butter over medium-low heat.
2. Add onion, leek, celery, garlic, and jalapeno chili. Cook, covered, for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
3. Stir in carrots, cabbage, potatoes, turnips, fennel, kohlrabi, celery root, broccoli, parsley, and enough stock to nearly cover the ingredients.
4. Bring contents to a boil; lower heat and simmer, uncovered, for 30 minutes or until very tender.
5. Puree the soup in batches using a processor or blender. Rinse out the pan and return the puréed soup to the saucepan.
6. Heat the soup gently over low heat. Stir in cream and season with salt and pepper to taste.
7. Serve garnished with sour cream and fresh chives.
NOTE:
At Thanksgiving dinner at The Four Seasons, this soup is served in a hollowed-out acorn squash. To serve it this way, slice off the tops and a thin sliver from the bottom of the acorn squash (so it can sit upright). Hollow out the insides, pour in the finished soup, and bake uncovered on a baking sheet at 375°F until the squash is tender (about 30 minutes). The squash will be tender enough to scrape from its shell and eat with the soup.
